summary_one_line: Reaching codeInteger's true branch needs a SELECT with integer-literal arithmetic expressions, not a trivial SELECT 1; constant.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A SELECT statement whose expression carries many ASCII integer literals joined by arithmetic operators (e.g. 0x35 '5', 0x2b '+', 0x2f '/', 0x2d '-'), driving codeInteger through the true branch at 102982:9.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winners are 108-171 byte SELECT bodies densely packed with digit '5' (0x35), '+' (0x2b), '/' (0x2f) and '-' (0x2d) bytes from offset 0x07 onward, exercising codeInteger 32 times on the true side (W L102982 T=32).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive losers are short SELECT 1; / garbage inputs (9-49 bytes) with no operator-joined literal run; they never take the true branch (L L102982 T=0 F=32).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching offsets, planting the integer-token byte patterns the parser/codegen compares against; the winner seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural feature (operator-joined integer-literal SELECT expression) reaching codeInteger's true branch, approached incrementally by closing CMP-operand distance.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolved 10/10 with avg hitcount 112 on the branch, far above the loser's 1, indicating it repeatedly reaches and takes the codeInteger codegen path.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive stays blocked 8/10 at the same branch with hitcount 1, never accumulating the literal-arithmetic structure needed.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP; an input that brings an integer-literal comparison closer to matching adds a new bucket and is preserved, providing a gradient toward the byte values the codegen branch requires.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same integer-literal SELECT-expression feature reaching codeInteger's true branch, found by spending more havoc mutations on small/fast/stable SELECT seeds.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer resolved 8/10 with avg hitcount 69 on the branch versus the loser's 1, reaching the codegen path far more often.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive's plain StdMutationalStage gives the same uniform energy to every seed and stays blocked 8/10 with hitcount 1.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer runs the same havoc+token stack inside a StdPowerMutationalStage whose CalibrationStage-derived perf_score grants faster, smaller, more-stable SELECT seeds more mutations per visit, raising the chance havoc assembles the required literal-arithmetic body.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true branch of codeInteger, SQLite's integer-literal codegen.
The blocked side is the true branch.
cmplog winners take the true branch at the blocker while naive losers do not.
cmplog winning seeds are SELECT bodies densely packed with '5','+','/','-' literal/operator bytes.
naive loser seeds are short trivial SELECT 1; inputs.
cmplog beats naive on this branch via I2S, prob_div 0.80.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile beats naive on this branch via the value_profile technique.
value_profile reaches the branch with avg hitcount 112 vs the loser's 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=112  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP preserves inputs that bring a CMP operand pair closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer beats naive on this branch via calibrated_energy.
minimizer reaches the branch with avg hitcount 69 vs the loser's 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=69  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer's power stage gives faster, smaller, more-stable seeds more mutations via calibration perf_score.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations

evidence_trail.claim: naive applies a uniform plain StdMutationalStage, unlike minimizer's StdPowerMutationalStage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
run inside a `StdPowerMutationalStage` rather than naive's
plain `StdMutationalStage`

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 14563 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 2bdff174433f9550`; the 28-level ancestor chain ends in two I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 26 and 27, the lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find under the current cmplog build. (Mechanism claim applies to the cmplog>naive (I2S) hypothesis; the other two hypotheses carry CMP_MAP gradient and calibrated energy mechanisms per multi_feature.)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog still fails to take codeInteger's true branch when the integer literal it codegens is replaced by a single fixed constant with no comparison against input bytes (no CMP for I2S to mine), while value_profile/minimizer also fail — showing the divergence is unrelated to integer-literal CMP introspection or energy.

weakest_evidence_point: The value_profile and minimizer mechanism attributions rest on hitcount/trial-vector divergence and generic technique knowledge rather than a direct byte-level CMP_MAP or per-seed energy trace at this specific branch.
